content.problemStatement:
There are NNN robots in a line, each with an assigned integer value. Each robot can terminate the closest robot either to its left or right. When a robot with value xxx terminates another robot with value yyy, the terminated robot disappears and the terminating robot's value changes to x−yx-yx−y. The process continues until only one robot remains.

Find the maximum possible value that the last remaining robot can have.


Input Format

First line contains a single integer NNN - the number of robots
Second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,...,ANA_1, A_2, ..., A_NA1​,A2​,...,AN​ - the values assigned to each robot


Output Format

Print a single integer - the maximum possible value of the last remaining robot

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Possible way:

Second robot terminates the third robot, the row now contains robots 2,-1,1
Second robot terminates the third robot, the row now contains robots 2,-2
First robot terminates the second robot, the row now contains 4

Explanation:
The first robot can keep terminating robots to its right to end up with a value of 4
